title: Overcoming public resistance to carbon taxes
authors: Stefano Carattini; Maria Carvalho; Sam Fankhauser

tax_mentions.context: Abstract and Introduction argue carbon taxes are cost-effective but politically resisted; the paper reviews acceptability determinants and design features.
Section 3.1 and Appendix A discuss acceptability of fuel taxes and gasoline tax increases; support falls as personal costs rise.
Sections 4-5 review how carbon tax revenue recycling, earmarking, lump-sum transfers, and rebates affect public support.

inclusion_reason: Review explicitly examines public acceptability, support, and preferences toward carbon taxes and fuel taxes, including tax rates, revenue recycling, earmarking, and redistribution.
